outlier_counts_note: outlier_pct is percentage of non-NULL values, not percentage of all rows. For high-NULL columns (e.g., feudal_age_uptime at 87% NULL), these differ substantially. See 'denominator' field in each entry.

near_constant_detection_note.justification: EDA Manual Section 3.3 uses uniqueness_ratio < 0.001 as a starting point. On a 30M/107M row dataset, this ratio flags continuous numerics with thousands of distinct values. Threshold of 100 separates genuinely low-cardinality columns (max categorical: map at 93) from continuous numerics (min: old_rating at 3,032). Columns flagged only by ratio are documented as 'near_constant_ratio_flagged' and are NOT near-constant.

fields.rationale: Lobby size known at match start
Game setting at lobby; near-constant (cardinality=2: 'dark' 30,690,632 rows vs 'standard' 19 rows)
Derivable from civ selections at match start
Game version known at match time
Team assignment known at match start
Player rating before match (authoritative pre-game signal)
LEAKAGE -- player rating after match (Invariant #3 violation if used as feature)

Leakage status unknown -- could be (new_rating - old_rating) = post_game, or (player_elo - opponent_elo) = pre_game. Requires empirical test: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM players_raw WHERE ABS(match_rating_diff - (new_rating - old_rating)) < 0.01
Time to Feudal Age, measured during gameplay; 87% NULL
Time to Castle Age, measured during gameplay; 88% NULL
Time to Imperial Age, measured during gameplay; 91% NULL
Replay data; available only after match via replay parsing

field_classification_note.match_rating_diff_leakage_test_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM players_raw WHERE ABS(match_rating_diff - (new_rating - old_rating)) < 0.01
field_classification_note.note: If result > 0, match_rating_diff encodes new_rating - old_rating = post_game (fatal leakage). If result == 0, may be pre_game differential. Run this test before Phase 02 feature engineering.

z_score_outliers_deferred.note: z-score outliers require mean/stddev; for highly skewed distributions (duration, age uptimes), z-scores are less informative than IQR fences. Full z-score profiling deferred to 01_03.

map.sql_null:
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS total_rows,
    COUNT(*) - COUNT("map") AS null_count,
    ROUND(100.0 * (COUNT(*) - COUNT("map")) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0), 4) AS null_pct,
    COUNT(DISTINCT "map") AS cardinality
FROM matches_raw
map.sql_top_n:
SELECT
    "map" AS value,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 4) AS pct
FROM matches_raw
GROUP BY "map"
ORDER BY cnt DESC, "map" ASC
LIMIT 5
map.sql_bottom_n:
SELECT
    "map" AS value,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 4) AS pct
FROM matches_raw
WHERE "map" IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY "map"
ORDER BY cnt ASC, "map" ASC
LIMIT 5

sql_queries.temporal_range:
SELECT
    MIN(started_timestamp) AS earliest,
    MAX(started_timestamp) AS latest,
    COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('month', started_timestamp)) AS distinct_months,
    COUNT(DISTINCT DATE_TRUNC('week', started_timestamp)) AS distinct_weeks
FROM matches_raw
sql_queries.monthly_match_counts:
SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', started_timestamp) AS month,
    COUNT(*) AS match_count
FROM matches_raw
WHERE started_timestamp IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month
sql_queries.orphan_detection:
SELECT
    'players_without_match' AS check_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT p.game_id) AS cnt
FROM players_raw p
LEFT JOIN matches_raw m ON p.game_id = m.game_id
WHERE m.game_id IS NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT
    'matches_without_players' AS check_name,
    COUNT(DISTINCT m.game_id) AS cnt
FROM matches_raw m
LEFT JOIN players_raw p ON m.game_id = p.game_id
WHERE p.game_id IS NULL
sql_queries.players_per_match:
SELECT
    player_count,
    COUNT(*) AS match_count,
    ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER(), 2) AS pct
FROM (
    SELECT game_id, COUNT(*) AS player_count
    FROM players_raw
    GROUP BY game_id
)
GROUP BY player_count
ORDER BY player_count
